summary: Native desktop auth uses explicit browser handoff, validated deep-link callbacks, bounded server session checks, and a paid-plan dashboard gate.

steps.render_launch_screen: The app initially renders a branded launch screen while auth startup begins.
steps.register_deep_link_listener: The deep-link listener is registered immediately and must not wait for saved-session validation to finish.
steps.start_startup_watchdog: A hard 5 second startup watchdog invalidates stale startup work and shows the web login screen if auth initialization has not completed.
steps.inspect_startup_deep_links: Startup deep-link inspection is bounded and handles only diffforge://auth/callback URLs with safe code and state values.
steps.restore_saved_session: A saved desktop session token is validated through the server before local state becomes authenticated.
steps.fallback_to_login: Missing, invalid, expired, stale, failed, or slow session checks clear local auth data and show the signed-out browser login action.
steps.start_web_login: Only an explicit user action creates a new random pending state and opens the hosted web login URL.
steps.handle_deep_link_callback: A callback must match the pending local state before any auth code exchange is attempted.
steps.exchange_one_time_code: The one-time desktop auth code exchange is bounded and saves only server-returned session data.
steps.persist_valid_session: The app stores the desktop session token and user snapshot only after a successful server response.
steps.gate_paid_dashboard: Authenticated desktop users see the dashboard only when server-returned user.planStatus is paid.
steps.refresh_plan_status: Plan refresh revalidates the stored desktop session and remains bounded so the pricing gate cannot hang.
steps.logout: Logout invalidates active local auth flow state, clears local session data, and attempts a bounded remote revoke.

conditions: The launch screen must never depend on any single unbounded Promise, plugin call, or Tauri command.
The startup launch screen must transition to either authenticated state or signed-out login within 5 seconds.
React StrictMode effect cleanup/setup cycles must not permanently skip auth initialization.
Late results from timed-out startup work must be ignored by comparing auth flow ids before mutating UI state.
Deep links must use the diffforge scheme, auth host, /callback path, and safe auth values before being considered.
Desktop auth codes are exchanged only when the returned state matches the locally pending state.
The browser login window is opened only after the user presses the sign-in button.
The dashboard must not render from cached user state without a successful server session validation or exchange.
Free, missing, unknown, stale, expired, or failed plan states must render the pricing/login surfaces, not the dashboard.
Remote logout failures must not keep a local session alive.
